---
title: "Protect Dependency Installs from Supply-Chain Attacks with a Release-Age Buffer"
subtitle: "Note#204"
id: "204"
date: 2026-04-01
date-modified: ""
date-format: iso
categories: [notes]
spotlight: ""
---
After the `litellm` incident on PyPI, and then `axios` on npm, I came across this trick on Twitter: configure your package manager to reject dependency releases that are too new.

## `uv`

```toml
# ~/.config/uv/uv.toml
exclude-newer = "7 days"
```

## `npm`

```ini
# ~/.npmrc
min-release-age=7 # days
ignore-scripts=true
```

## `bun`

```toml
# ~/.bunfig.toml
[install]
minimumReleaseAge = 604800 # seconds
```

## Related Notes

* [Note#126 – Managing Pinned Dependencies for Python Packages with `uv`](https://sderev.com/notes/126)

## References

* [TIL: `uv` settings after `litellm`](https://antisimplistic.com/posts/2026-03-28-til-uv-settings-after-litellm/)
